channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada for Tuesday, while doubling existing 10% tariffs on China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: HUD terminates Obama-era housing rule that Trump warned would 'destroy' home values
item.description: The Trump administration rescinded the Affirmatively Furthering Fair Housing (AFFH) rule, which mandated that localities commission extensive analyses to certify that new housing developments do not exacerbate disparities among federally protected groups.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Giving US ownership of Ukraine's minerals would help keep peace: Hassett
item.description: Kevin Hassett said in an interview with NewsNation's Kellie Meyer that these minerals' estimated worth is $20B.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE access to US intelligence secrets poses a national security threat, Democrats say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic lawmakers are demanding answers from Elon Musk about whether staffers at his Department of Government Efficiency have shared national security secrets over insecure communication channels. Sen. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts and Rep.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Trump administration kills nearly all USAID programs
item.description: The Trump administration is terminating thousands of foreign assistance grants and awards, according to a court filing. The move effectively guts the six-decade-old agency.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis and Frank Langfitt, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin won’t tolerate antisemitism in schools, says Leo Terrell as NYC schools under microscope
item.description: Antisemitism in New York City's public schools has continued through biased curricula, politically active teachers and the mislabeling of reported incidents of antisemitic hate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesotan rehired after losing Forest Service job in Colorado
item.description: Emma Schultz, who is originally from Roseville, was terminated from her position — but a supervisor notified her over the phone that she had been terminated by mistake because her job had been deemed “mission critical.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Musk and his 'humble tech support' effort get star turn at Trump's Cabinet meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk took a star turn at the first Cabinet meeting of President Donald Trump's new term, holding forth in a black “Make America Great Again” campaign hat on Wednesday about his role as “humble tech support” for the federal government — and laying out dire stakes if his cost-cu
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Jamieson Greer to be Trump's top trade negotiator as battles loom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate has confirmed Jamieson Greer, a veteran of President Donald Trump's first-term economic battles with China, Mexico and Canada, to be America's top trade negotiator. As U.S.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump introduces a green card for the rich: the gold card
item.description: The new visa program would effectively replace the current EB-5 investor visa program, and the proceeds could be used for deficit reduction.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Cicero Primary Election: Incumbent Dominick defeats Rodriguez, will serve 6th straight term
item.description: Incumbent Cicero Town President Larry Dominick will serve a fifth straight four-year term, defeating Esteban Rodriguez in the primary election Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Dana Rebik, WGN 9

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: “We're going to be selling a gold card. You have a green card. This is a gold card. We're going to be putting a price on that card of about $5 million and that's going to give you green card privileges,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Federal watchdog agency intervenes in Trump’s purge of probationary employees
item.description: U.S. Special Counsel Hampton Dellinger has asked the Merit Systems Protection Board to temporarily reinstate six federal employees fired from their jobs and is considering ways to seek relief for others.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: FCC chairman climbs Nexstar broadcast tower to promote industry, jobs
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, Fox 5

item.title: Musk says failure to respond to email a second time will end in termination
item.description: The message appears to contradict recent guidance from the Office of Personnel Management (OPM), which told agency leaders Monday afternoon that employee response to the initial email was not mandatory and that failure to do so would not be considered a resignation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Roosevelt Hotel to shut down as migrant arrival center: NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: MANHATTAN, N.Y. (PIX11) – The migrant arrival center at the Roosevelt Hotel in Manhattan is set to shut down, New York City Mayor Eric Adams will announce on Monday. More than 173,000 migrants have gone through the site since it opened in May 2023, according to city data.
item.dc:creator: Kala Rama, PIX 11

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: PIX Panel discusses Hochul’s safeguard plan for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Thursday announced proposed safeguards to ensure that New York City is operating in the best interest of its residents.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
